channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chirag Paswan Criticises Bihar Government Amid Public Rally
item.description: On 26th July, Union Minister Chirag Paswan addressed a public gathering in Gaya, Bihar, where he openly criticised the Nitish Kumar-led NDA government. Speaking in the wake of the ambulance rape incid...

item.title: Vatican criticizes US-Israeli military actions in Iran as a threat to international law
item.description: Vatican Secretary of State Cardinal Pietro Parolin has strongly criticized the ongoing US-Israeli military strikes on Iran, calling them 'truly alarming' for their impact on international law. In an u...

item.title: Iran’s Low-Cost Drones Pose Financial Challenge to US Defence Systems
item.description: The United States is currently dominating the skies over Iran, but the cost dynamics of modern drone warfare are proving challenging. Iran has deployed over 2,000 low-cost Shahed drones since the US a...

item.title: EAC to review Partner States’ contributions based on economic capacity
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) Council of Ministers has been directed to review the current contribution formula used by Partner States. The present model requires each country to contribute $7 mill...

item.title: March Fuel Price Update: Petrol, Diesel, and Kerosene Increase Across Coastal Ports
item.description: Starting March 4th, 2026, fuel prices in Tanzania have increased across all major coastal ports. In Dar es Salaam, petrol now costs Sh2,864 per litre, up Sh76 from February. Diesel experienced a sharp...

item.title: JNIM jihadists launch coordinated attacks in northern and eastern Burkina Faso
item.description: Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM), the al-Qaeda linked group, has carried out a series of deadly attacks in northern and eastern Burkina Faso in recent days. These operations targeted milita...

item.title: Dentists in England return hundreds of millions to NHS amid private care prioritisation
item.description: Dentists in England have returned over £900 million to the NHS over the past two years due to unfulfilled NHS contracts, equating to approximately £1 for every £7 received. The system requires dentist...

item.title: Supply chain finance key to unlocking intra-African trade under AfCFTA
item.description: African businesses, particularly small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), often find cross-border trade within the continent riskier than exporting overseas. Challenges include limited information o...

item.title: 18-Year-Old Newlywed Woman Found Dead on Rooftop in Karachi DHA
item.description: KARACHI: In a tragic incident, the body of an 18-year-old newly married woman, Zainab, was found hanging on the rooftop of a residential building in Defence Housing Authority (DHA) Phase V, Karachi, d...

item.title: Race-by-race tips and previews for Randwick's Saturday meeting
item.description: The Canberra Times provides a detailed guide to Saturday's major race meeting at Randwick, offering tips and race previews from the expert Ray Hickson. The article highlights key contenders in each of...

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang explains decision to limit future investments in OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: At the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media and Telecom conference, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced that the company's recent investments in OpenAI and Anthropic are likely to be its last in both firms....

item.title: Research Reveals How Zebrafish Larval Behavior Sheds Light on Human Handedness
item.description: Handedness, whether right or left, is a familiar aspect of daily life that shapes how humans perform countless tasks, from writing to eating. Surprisingly, this behavioral asymmetry is not unique to h...

item.title: Loco pilot finally granted medical leave after showing unhealed surgery wound
item.description: In a disturbing incident from Lucknow Rail Division, a loco pilot named Rajesh Meena, belonging to the Scheduled Tribe community, was reportedly denied sick leave even after undergoing surgery for pil...

item.title: The Role of Female Friendships in Supporting Women’s Success
item.description: Success is often portrayed as a personal journey driven by ambition and hard work, but research shows that female friendships play a critical role in supporting women’s achievements. From childhood th...

item.title: UK government-chartered flight from Oman for stranded Britons delayed due to check-in issues and pilot rest requirements
item.description: The inaugural UK government-chartered flight intended to repatriate British nationals from the Middle East, amid the escalating conflict involving Iran, the US, and Israel, failed to depart from Musca...

item.title: Qatar Airways cancels F1 hospitality events in Melbourne amid flight disruptions
item.description: Qatar Airways, the naming sponsor of the Australian Grand Prix and a 25 per cent stakeholder in Virgin Australia, has cancelled all its hospitality events in Melbourne this weekend. Originally, the ai...

item.title: LTO cancels licenses of seven drivers after alcohol-related violations
item.description: The Land Transportation Office (LTO) has officially revoked the driving licenses of seven individuals found guilty of drunk driving. According to the LTO Intelligence and Investigation Division, all s...

item.title: House Justice Committee Requires Vice President Sara Duterte to Respond to Verified Impeachment Complaints
item.description: The House Committee on Justice has officially directed Vice President Sara Duterte to respond to verified impeachment complaints filed against her. On March 4, 2026, the committee served a Notice to R...

item.title: Keith Olbermann Faces Backlash for Derogatory Comment on Lou Holtz Following Coach's Death at 89
item.description: Legendary college football coach Lou Holtz, renowned for leading Notre Dame to an undefeated national championship in 1988 and earning the Presidential Medal of Freedom from former President Donald Tr...

item.title: Key Peace and Security Challenges Africa Must Address in 2026
item.description: Africa dey face plenty wahala for peace and security matter as 2026 don start. Big conflicts dey for Sudan, Sahel, Somalia, South Sudan, Cameroon, Ethiopia-Eritrea, and even the eastern DRC. For Sudan...
